name: Orientation & Activity Fee
description: Orientation, academic briefings, workshops and campus activities

name: Facilities & Resource Fee
description: Maintenance of infrastructure, e-learning, library, internet, utilities, transport, sports facilities

name: Laboratory/Workshop Fee
description: Lab equipment, software licenses, consumables, maintenance

name: Special Administration Fee
description: Administrative work for accreditation by professional bodies

name: International Student Administrative Fee (ISAF)
description: Visa, entry pass, accommodation, transport, liaison services

name: Status Maintenance Fee
description: Maintain student status after normal study duration

text: Estimated total fees may vary by up to 10 percent
Additional fees may apply for dual awards, materials, field trips, and accreditation
Fees subject to change without prior notice

name: Lee Xuen
content: Studying abroad was a life-changing decision that helped me gain new perspectives and develop cross-cultural awareness. My journey began on 27 May 2018, and although I was nervous leaving my family, it was an exciting experience. Studying in an English-speaking country improved my communication skills significantly. I am grateful for supportive lecturers and friends who helped me overcome challenges. This experience has made me a better person and will always remain unforgettable.

name: Roselyn Chua Song Hui
content: In summer 2018, I had the opportunity to study in the UK, which was an exciting experience. I learned about British culture and traveled across the UK and Europe. The teaching style encouraged independence and active participation, improving my communication and public speaking skills. The university facilities were excellent, including a 24-hour library and academic support services. Despite culture shock and homesickness, the experience helped me grow into a more mature individual.

name: Nicholas Kuan
content: Joining the study abroad programme at Coventry University was one of the best decisions I have made. Experiencing a different culture expanded my worldview. The lecturers were friendly and dedicated, and the people I met were helpful throughout my stay. I look forward to visiting Coventry again in the future.

name: Shahrul Ariffin
content: Studying abroad enriched my university experience and broadened my worldview. The university had excellent facilities and interactive teaching methods that encouraged participation. These approaches helped develop my critical thinking skills, which are valuable for the future.

name: Leong Shui Xin
content: Studying abroad enhanced my global skills and networking opportunities, which are important for employability. Although adapting to a new culture was sometimes overwhelming, it contributed to significant personal growth. Group work and travel experiences helped maintain a balance between academics and social life.

name: Rene Lai
content: I always wanted to study abroad, and this opportunity allowed me to experience a new culture beyond what is possible in Malaysia. Working with international peers was challenging but rewarding. I made lifelong friends from many countries and traveled across the UK and Europe. The experience was unforgettable and filled with lasting memories.

name: Lim Teck Yu
content: Studying at Napier was one of the best decisions I have made. The city is beautiful, and the academic staff were supportive both academically and personally. Living abroad helped me develop independence, adaptability, and lifelong friendships. It was a truly meaningful experience.

name: Joan Loo Kit Yee
content: As part of the dual award programme, I gained valuable international exposure and built meaningful connections. The campus location offered stunning views, and transportation in Switzerland was convenient. Small class sizes allowed for personalized learning and effective interaction with lecturers. Studying abroad helped me become more independent and broadened my perspective.

name: Wong Jia Hang
content: Although I initially hesitated, studying at IMI turned out to be a decision I never regretted. Despite the challenges of the COVID-19 lockdown, the institution provided strong support for international students. The experience was valuable and memorable.

name: Au Yong Yin Yi
content: My experience at IMI was enjoyable, with engaging and interactive classes. Although travel restrictions limited sightseeing opportunities, I still had a positive experience. The institution provided clear guidance and strong support for international students, making it easier to adapt to life away from home.

name: Jonathan
content: Studying abroad was a life-changing experience that broadened my horizons and helped me develop cross-cultural awareness. The support from the Study Abroad Unit (SAU) at TAR UMT was instrumental in making the application process smooth and stress-free. My academic experience in Australia was positive, with engaging and interactive classes that encouraged critical thinking. I also had the opportunity to participate in field trips, which provided practical learning experiences. The campus environment was enjoyable, and I made many friends from around the world. Overall, studying abroad has been an unforgettable experience that has enriched my personal and academic growth.

name: Tan Yi Siang
content: Progressing from TAR UMT to Birmingham City University for further studies was a significant step in my academic journey. Studying abroad allowed me to develop independence and adaptability as I navigated a new culture and academic environment. The university provided strong academic resources and facilities that supported my learning. Additionally, I had the opportunity to travel and explore the UK, which enriched my overall experience. The support from the SAU during the application and visa process was invaluable, making the transition smoother. Studying abroad has been a rewarding experience that has contributed to my personal growth and career progression.

name: Tong Wei Lee
content: Studying abroad at the Communication University of China was a transformative experience that provided me with valuable insights into a different culture and academic system. The guidance from the SAU at TAR UMT was crucial in helping me make informed decisions about my study destination. Transitioning from an engineering background to business studies was challenging but rewarding, as it allowed me to gain clarity on my career direction. The mentorship and continuous support from counsellors were instrumental in helping me adapt to life away from home and build confidence in making important life decisions. Overall, studying abroad has been a meaningful experience that has broadened my perspective and enhanced my personal growth.

name: On-Campus Hostel
notes: No WiFi in rooms; students use own data. Free WiFi available in canteen and study rooms only.

name: Off-Campus Accommodation
application_notes: Students must arrange directly with landlord, tenant, or agent. University is not responsible for disputes or issues.
listing_info: Property owners can advertise by submitting form and identification to the Department of Student Affairs.

institution: Tunku Abdul Rahman University of Management and Technology
address: Ground Floor, Bangunan Tan Sri Khaw Kai Boh (Block A), Jalan Genting Kelang, 53300 Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

data.notes: No WiFi in rooms; WiFi available in study areas and canteen.
Students arrange directly with landlords; university not responsible for disputes.
Application considered complete only after documents submitted and fee paid. Applicants notified via email and SMS.
Some documents can be submitted later but must be verified before admission is finalized.

data.bachelor_degree: A Level with minimum 2 Grade D or equivalent
data.diploma: O Level/IGCSE with minimum 3 Grade C
data.foundation: O Level/IGCSE with minimum 5 Grade C including English
data.notes: Requirements must be from a single sitting and meeting minimum does not guarantee admission.
Some programmes require higher English proficiency. Exemption possible for students from English-speaking countries.
Routes connect campus to nearby housing areas and LRT stations.
Application via intranet. Only university issues passes.
No local tuition fees during study abroad semester. Fees subject to change.

data.note: Routes connect campus with nearby housing and transport hubs.
Applications submitted via university intranet only.
No local tuition fees during study abroad semester. Fees subject to change.
Some programmes are phased out and no longer available for new intake.
Students must complete co-curricular credits to graduate.

data.summary: The university provides a vibrant campus environment focused on holistic student development beyond academics, emphasizing skills, values, and career readiness.

data.notes: Suitable for business travelers and short-term stays within campus.

data.address: Jalan Genting Kelang, Setapak, 53300 Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
